headline: Nvidia Earnings Likely to Shine Even as Stock Underperforms
summary: Artificial-intelligence leader Nvidia has a big task when it reports first-quarter earnings later today: convince investors that the AI investment boom still has legs and that Nvidia won’t lose its pole position.  Wall Street analysts expect the company to report earnings per share of $1.75, up from 81 cents a year ago.  Nvidia remains the biggest winner of the AI age.

headline: Exclusive-Meta CEO tells employees he does not expect more company-wide layoffs this year
summary: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg told employees in an internal ‌memo on Wednesday that he does not expect more ‌company-wide layoffs this year, according to a copy of the memo ​seen by Reuters.  He made the announcement on the same day the Facebook owner carried out a massive restructuring of the company, laying off 10% of its workforce globally and ‌transferring 7,000 other ⁠employees to new initiatives related to AI workflows.  The changes are part of a far-reaching overhaul taking place at Meta this year, as the company surges its AI investments in a bid to center AI ‌agents in both its product offerings ​and its approach to work ​internally.

content.title: Exclusive-Meta CEO tells employees he does not expect more company-wide layoffs this year
content.summary: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg told employees in an internal ‌memo on Wednesday that he does not expect more ‌company-wide layoffs this year, according to a copy of the memo ​seen by Reuters.  He made the announcement on the same day the Facebook owner carried out a massive restructuring of the company, laying off 10% of its workforce globally and ‌transferring 7,000 other ⁠employees to new initiatives related to AI workflows.  The changes are part of a far-reaching overhaul taking place at Meta this year, as the company surges its AI investments in a bid to center AI ‌agents in both its product offerings ​and its approach to work ​internally.

headline: India's Nykaa seeks to include Meta in music copyright fight with Zee
summary: Indian fashion-to-beauty retailer Nykaa has asked a New Delhi court ‌to make Meta a party to a copyright ‌dispute filed by media firm Zee, court documents show, in a case ​seen as having implications for the commercial use of music on Instagram.  • Zee Entertainment has sued Nykaa for allegedly using its copyrighted songs in Instagram reels to promote its products, ‌seeking $210,000 in damages, ⁠Reuters reported this month.  • In a non-public filing dated May 19 and seen by Reuters ⁠on Wednesday, Nykaa told the Delhi High Court that Meta must be included, as only it can "authoritatively state whether use" ​of music ​clips by Nykaa breached any ​licence terms.

content.title: India's Nykaa seeks to include Meta in music copyright fight with Zee
content.summary: Indian fashion-to-beauty retailer Nykaa has asked a New Delhi court ‌to make Meta a party to a copyright ‌dispute filed by media firm Zee, court documents show, in a case ​seen as having implications for the commercial use of music on Instagram.  • Zee Entertainment has sued Nykaa for allegedly using its copyrighted songs in Instagram reels to promote its products, ‌seeking $210,000 in damages, ⁠Reuters reported this month.  • In a non-public filing dated May 19 and seen by Reuters ⁠on Wednesday, Nykaa told the Delhi High Court that Meta must be included, as only it can "authoritatively state whether use" ​of music ​clips by Nykaa breached any ​licence terms.

headline: Dollar Gains, Tied to Iran War, May Test Stock Rally Over the Coming Weeks
summary: The U.S. dollar has been quietly making a sustained advance on global currency markets amid a surge in global energy prices and a stunning comeback rally in the stock market.  The dollar’s gains, in fact, could extend over the coming weeks, and start to weigh on domestic stock performance over the back half of the year, with the Iran war effectively closing the Strait of Hormuz and sending oil prices higher.  The U.S. dollar index, which tracks the greenback against a basket of six major global currency peers, was marked 0.06% higher on Wednesday and trading at 99.03, nearly at levels seen in early April.

content.title: Dollar Gains, Tied to Iran War, May Test Stock Rally Over the Coming Weeks
content.summary: The U.S. dollar has been quietly making a sustained advance on global currency markets amid a surge in global energy prices and a stunning comeback rally in the stock market.  The dollar’s gains, in fact, could extend over the coming weeks, and start to weigh on domestic stock performance over the back half of the year, with the Iran war effectively closing the Strait of Hormuz and sending oil prices higher.  The U.S. dollar index, which tracks the greenback against a basket of six major global currency peers, was marked 0.06% higher on Wednesday and trading at 99.03, nearly at levels seen in early April.

headline: Meta CEO tells employees he does not expect more company-wide layoffs this year
summary: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg told employees in an internal memo on ‌Wednesday that he does not expect more company-wide layoffs ‌this year, according to a copy of the memo seen by Reuters.  He made ​the announcement on the same day the Facebook owner carried out a massive restructuring of the company, laying off 10% of its workforce globally and transferring 7,000 other employees to new initiatives ‌related to AI workflows.  Employees ‌left comments on his post quoting the words "company-wide" and "expect."

content.title: Meta CEO tells employees he does not expect more company-wide layoffs this year
content.summary: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg told employees in an internal memo on ‌Wednesday that he does not expect more company-wide layoffs ‌this year, according to a copy of the memo seen by Reuters.  He made ​the announcement on the same day the Facebook owner carried out a massive restructuring of the company, laying off 10% of its workforce globally and transferring 7,000 other employees to new initiatives ‌related to AI workflows.  Employees ‌left comments on his post quoting the words "company-wide" and "expect."

thumbnail.caption: FILE PHOTO: Meta Platforms CEO Mark Zuckerberg arrives outside court to take the stand at trial, in Los Angeles

headline: Why embracing AI may be the key to career survival
summary: Glassdoor Senior Economist Daniel Zhao joins Brooke DiPalma on Market Domination to discuss employee confidence as companies continue trimming headcount and accelerating AI-driven workplace changes. Zhao explains why workers who embrace AI tools and adapt their skill sets may be better positioned for long-term job security.

content.title: Why embracing AI may be the key to career survival

Zhao explains why workers who embrace AI tools and adapt their skill sets may be better positioned for long-term job security.

headline: Anthropic revenue set to more than double to $10.9 billion in Q2
summary: Investing.com -- Anthropic is projected to more than double its revenue from $4.8 billion in the first quarter to $10.9 billion in the second quarter, according to a report from the Wall Street Journal, citing figures shared during an ongoing funding round.

content.title: Anthropic revenue set to more than double to $10.9 billion in Q2
content.summary: Investing.com -- Anthropic is projected to more than double its revenue from $4.8 billion in the first quarter to $10.9 billion in the second quarter, according to a report from the Wall Street Journal, citing figures shared during an ongoing funding round.

headline: Nvidia's Data Center Revenue Soars. It's Not Just From the 'Hyperscalers.'
summary: Nvidia's first-quarter data center sales soared by 92% from last year to $75 billion, a new record.  Nvidia also changed how it breaks up data center sales in its reporting.  It's now showing how much of it comes from outside of the so-called "hyperscalers" of Amazon.com, Microsoft, Alphabet, and Meta Platforms.

content.title: Nvidia's Data Center Revenue Soars. It's Not Just From the 'Hyperscalers.'
content.summary: Nvidia's first-quarter data center sales soared by 92% from last year to $75 billion, a new record.  Nvidia also changed how it breaks up data center sales in its reporting.  It's now showing how much of it comes from outside of the so-called "hyperscalers" of Amazon.com, Microsoft, Alphabet, and Meta Platforms.

headline: Intuit Plans to Cut 17% of Workforce, But Isn’t Blaming AI
summary: Intuit  reported better-than-expected financial results for its crucial tax season Wednesday, while also announcing a round of layoffs.  Intuit  said it’s reducing its full-time workforce by 17%.  According to Layoffs.fyi, a website that tracks tech layoffs, 111,173 tech employees have lost their jobs in 2026.

headline: These 2 AI Giants Have Been Dormant. That’s Exactly Why They Might Surprise Investors
summary: The AI trade has had favorites. NVIDIA, Microsoft, Alphabet, and Meta have carried the narrative, while two enterprise tech giants have quietly compounded earnings and built backlogs that few retail investors are talking about. With the S&P 500 up 8% year to date, the setup for these laggards looks asymmetric heading into the back half ... These 2 AI Giants Have Been Dormant. That’s Exactly Why They Might Surprise Investors

headline: Zuckerberg says he feels 'weight' of Meta layoffs
summary: Meta began laying off roughly 8,000 employees Wednesday -- about 10 percent of its global workforce -- as co-founder and Chief Executive Mark Zuckerberg pushes to redirect resources toward an ambitious artificial intelligence agenda.In a memo to staff Wednesday, posted by Business Insider, Zuckerberg expressed thanks to departing employees and sought to reassure those remaining.

content.title: Zuckerberg says he feels 'weight' of Meta layoffs
content.summary: Meta began laying off roughly 8,000 employees Wednesday -- about 10 percent of its global workforce -- as co-founder and Chief Executive Mark Zuckerberg pushes to redirect resources toward an ambitious artificial intelligence agenda.In a memo to staff Wednesday, posted by Business Insider, Zuckerberg expressed thanks to departing employees and sought to reassure those remaining.
